name: Preselection
summary: Default options pre-checked in favor of the business rather than the user.

evidence.claim: Preselection is listed in Brignull's canonical deceptive-patterns taxonomy.

body.description: A checkbox, radio, or toggle is defaulted to the option that benefits the business (accept cookies, subscribe to newsletter, enable marketing, opt-in to data sharing, add protection plan) — relying on inattention to skip past (E1).

detectionHeuristics.description: Pre-checked controls — verify whether the associated option advantages the business.

body.llmVerifyPrompt: For each pre-checked checkbox or radio, identify who benefits from the default (user, business, both, neither). Flag those benefiting the business.
body.remediation: Highlight all pre-checked options. Recommend the user explicitly reconsider each before submitting.
